div.head: The Consul at Geneva (Gilbert) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 363. I learn from an authoritative source that the Spanish delegation has submitted to the Secretary General documentary evidence of violations of the non-intervention agreement by Germany, Italy and Portugal. So far this evidence has not been made publicPrinted by the Spanish Government and circulated, on September 30, 1936, to all the delegations at Geneva and to the press. Copies of Spanish White Paper. La “non intervention” dans les Affaires d’Espagne: Documents public’s par le Gouvernement de la République Espagnole, transmitted to the Department with despatch No. X–27, November 8, 1936, from the Embassy in Madrid, not printed. (852.00/3914) and it is not yet clear what developments may result from its submission to the Secretariat.
